acceptance_criteria:
**Acceptance**
- [ ] IPC request structs no longer expose raw `String` for identifiers/namespace/actor/branch/durability.
- [ ] Invalid IDs/namespaces/branches are rejected during deserialization (before daemon logic).
- [ ] All daemon parsing code that manually validates these fields is removed or reduced to unavoidable businessŌĆælogic checks.
- [ ] Tests cover IPC decode failure on invalid IDs and success on valid inputs.
description:
**Problem**
IPC payloads use raw strings for IDs, namespaces, actors, durability, and branch names. That defers parsing/validation to adŌĆæhoc runtime logic in the daemon, which means mistakes are not caught by the compiler and invalid payloads can slip through if a parse call is missed.

Examples:
- `MutationMeta.namespace`, `actor_id`, `durability` are `Option<String>`.
- `IdPayload`, `DepPayload`, `CreatePayload.parent`, etc. are raw `String`.
- `ClosePayload.on_branch` is `Option<String>`.

Key refs:
- `crates/beads-surface/src/ipc/types.rs:18`
- `crates/beads-surface/src/ipc/payload.rs:12`

**Impact**
ParseŌĆædonŌĆÖtŌĆævalidate is violated at the IPC boundary. Compiler cannot enforce that IDs and namespaces are valid.
design:
**Design (opinionated)**
Parse at the boundary. Replace raw strings with typed core IDs and enforce validation in serde.

- Use `BeadId`, `NamespaceId`, `ActorId`, `BranchName`, `DurabilityClass` in IPC structs.
- Provide custom serde/`TryFrom<String>` (already exists for most core IDs) so invalid inputs fail deserialization.
- If backward compatibility requires strings on the wire, keep the wire format as strings but decode into typed fields immediately.

This makes ŌĆ£forgot to validateŌĆØ a compileŌĆætime error and rejects invalid inputs early.
title: Parse IPC payloads into typed IDs at boundary

description:
**Problem**
Clock behavior is hard to test deterministically. We should be able to inject time sources to simulate skew and verify monotonicity across restarts.

**Design**
Introduce a TimeSource trait for Clock (SystemTimeSource default). Allow tests to inject a fake source. Add tests covering backward/forward jumps and ensure stamps are monotonic and skew is detected.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Clock supports injected time source in tests
- [ ] Monotonicity tests cover backward/forward jumps
- [ ] No API changes to production callers (Clock::new remains default)

**Files:** src/daemon/clock.rs, src/daemon/core.rs
title: Clock time-source injection + monotonic tests

description:
**Problem**
`bd sync` cannot create or update `refs/heads/beads/store` over some SSH remotes even when plain `git fetch`/`git push` work from the same repo. Repro: `/Users/darin/src/personal/papers_rs` had a local `refs/heads/beads/store`, no remote store ref, and `bd sync` wedged until timeout; a direct `git push origin refs/heads/beads/store:refs/heads/beads/store` succeeded immediately. The current `beads-git` sync paths use libgit2 SSH-agent callbacks directly, which diverge from the user's working git SSH transport.

**Design**
Make SSH store transport use a path that matches normal git auth behavior for fetch/push/init, instead of depending solely on libgit2's SSH-agent callback path. Keep refspecs and sync semantics unchanged.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] SSH remotes that work with plain `git fetch`/`git push` also work for `bd sync` store transport
- [ ] Missing remote `beads/store` ref does not block initial SSH sync when git can create it
- [ ] Existing file/local-path remote tests stay green
- [ ] Add regression coverage for SSH transport selection/behavior

**Files:** `crates/beads-git/src/sync.rs`, tests near the owning crate
title: SSH store sync should honor normal git auth behavior
